commit_scope: Add same-browser meeting-shape fields to anonymous meeting traces without cross-device matching or fingerprint-like expansion.

commands: cd web && npm test -- tests/anonymous-meeting-trace.test.ts (first pass found missing fresh-worktree dependency: vitest)
cd api && python3 -m ruff check app/routers/meetings.py app/services/anonymous_meeting_trace_service.py tests/test_anonymous_meeting_traces.py
git fetch origin main && git rebase --autostash origin/main
local_validation.summary: Anonymous meeting traces now expose entry surface, ordered surface sequence, page count, coarse duration bucket, and hostname-only referrer domains. The browser sends start/end timestamps and external referrer hostname only. No IP address, geolocation, user agent, raw local keys, full referrer URLs, or cross-device matching were added. Local PR guard passed with ready_for_push=True and stale PR follow-through found zero stale Codex PRs.

ci_validation.details: Pending push, PR checks, and merge verification.

deploy_validation.details: Public API/web verification will be performed after merge and deploy.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: A returned same-browser source point can now be understood by meeting shape: first surface, path sequence, page count, coarse duration, and coarse external origin domain.
test_flows: post /come-in with referrer domain -> entry surface, sequence, page count, duration bucket, referrer domain
post /vision with same source/session -> ordered surface sequence and merged referrer domains
post returning session with contributor_id -> summary folds prior same-browser meetings into contributor

phase_gate.reason: Local proof is complete; commit, push, PR, checks, merge, and public verification remain pending.
